info.title: Refindery
info.description: A local retrieval engine over the web pages you read. Returns ranked, grounded passages with provenance; synthesis is the caller's job.

post.description: Add a page the user read to the index. body_extracted and body_html are mutually exclusive; when neither is given the URL is fetched asynchronously. Known canonical URLs record a revisit instead.
Validate and ingest each page independently. Results preserve input order; malformed or blacklisted items do not prevent other items.

post.summary: Fetch status for up to 500 pages
post.description: Return one status per distinct requested page id. Unknown ids are reported with found=false instead of failing the batch.

get.description: Return the full stored body text and metadata of one page from the user's reading history.
Return chunk text and body offsets in ordinal order.
Return the indexing lifecycle status of a page.

List jobs newest first, optionally filtered by status and kind.

``status_filter`` is a deprecated compatibility alias for ``status``; a
request that supplies both is rejected.

post.description: Retry dead jobs either by explicit `job_ids` (up to 500, deduped, results in input order) or by selector (all dead jobs, optionally filtered by `kind`, up to `limit`). Non-dead jobs are reported as skipped and unknown ids as not_found; the call is idempotent — a second selector call finds nothing left to retry. Always returns 200.
Reset a dead job to pending and re-enqueue it; other states return 409.

Hybrid semantic + keyword search over pages the user has read. Returns grounded passages from the user's own reading history. Contains no information the user has not read. Returns an empty result when nothing matches.

post.summary: Search many queries in one call
post.description: Run up to 20 queries sequentially with shared parameters (sequential on purpose: it respects embedding provider rate limits, like model comparison does). Each item gets its own query_id and query-log row, so feedback and evaluation join per query. Per-item failures are reported inline as outcome='error' items; the call itself stays 200.

get.description: Rank other pages from the user's reading history by similarity to this one. Returns grounded results from the user's own reading history only.

post.description: Append feedback; unknown query_ids are accepted (eval join drops them).

post.summary: Purge and blacklist a URL or domain
post.description: Permanently remove matching pages from the index and blacklist the pattern so future ingests are rejected. This deletes user data; removal of the blacklist rule later does not restore content.

get.description: All rules, newest first.

delete.description: Future ingests are allowed again; purged content stays purged.

get.description: Topic clusters discovered over the user's reading history. Returns grounded results derived only from pages the user has read.
Return persisted clustering history newest first.

get.summary: Read a run's two-dimensional page projection
get.description: Return page coordinates for a persisted clustering run.

get.summary: A cluster's label, keywords, and member pages
get.description: Member pages of one topic cluster from the user's reading history. Tombstoned clusters remain resolvable by id.

post.description: Enqueue a manual run; 409 when one is already in flight.

get.description: Resolve an entity by id, canonical form, or alias (references survive merges). Returns aliases and the user's pages mentioning it — grounded in the reading history only.
Entities linked to one page.

post.description: Restore a merged entity from its snapshot.

get.description: Return durable progress for a model's corpus backfill.

post.description: confirm=false returns an exact dry-run estimate (chunk token counts are stored); confirm=true starts the durable, resumable backfill.

get.description: All registered models.

post.description: Register (not active, no vectors yet). Rejected below the chunk budget.

post.summary: Make this the active search model
post.description: Atomic flip; only ready models can be activated.

delete.summary: Retire a model and drop its vector space
delete.description: 409 when active; drops the vector space and page vectors.

post.summary: Compare embedding models on one query
post.description: Runs the full retrieval pipeline once per model; the sparse arm and reranker are identical across arms, so the delta isolates the embedder. Returns per-model rankings over the user's reading history plus agreement statistics (Jaccard@k, RBO, Kendall's tau).

get.description: All watches with their schedules and last-poll health (status, error, item count).

post.description: Register a URL to poll on a schedule (default every 24 hours). Each poll discovers the source's current item URLs and ingests the new ones, so they become searchable. Kind 'rss' polls an RSS/Atom feed; kind 'youtube' polls a YouTube playlist or channel and ingests each video's transcript (single videos go to add_page instead). Already-indexed and blacklisted items are skipped automatically. The first poll runs within a minute of creation.

get.description: One watch with its schedule and last-poll health.

patch.description: Partially update a watch: pause/resume with 'enabled', change the poll interval, title, or per-kind config. Omitted fields are left unchanged; the URL is immutable (delete and recreate instead). Changing the interval reschedules the next poll from the last run.

delete.description: Stop polling this source. Pages already ingested by the watch stay indexed; use forget to remove content.

post.description: Enqueue an immediate poll instead of waiting for the schedule; the next scheduled poll moves to one interval from now. Returns the poll job id; track it via the jobs API.

get.description: Read historical rollups plus current gauge values for one metric.

One JSON snapshot of operational health: job-ledger counts, vector tombstone backlog, dropped observability rows, provider error counters, search latency quantiles, and circuit breaker states. Counter-derived fields are process-lifetime totals that reset on restart; `since` bounds the latency window only.

List retrieval traces newest first, optionally filtered by time and kind.

Each trace includes candidate, dense, sparse, and final hit sets, per-stage
timing, and the latest relevance feedback. The default limit is 100 and the
maximum is 1,000.
Return one full retrieval trace with rankings, timing, and feedback.

post.description: Score logged rankings synchronously without calling external providers.

Enqueue a durable two-arm replay that may call paid model providers.

The response is a 202 with a job ID and result URL. Polling the result needs
only read scope; the report or failure survives process restarts.

get.description: Read durable replay status, its completed report, or terminal failure.

Return effective settings with secrets redacted recursively.

Every setting is marked ``boot_only`` because runtime mutation is not
supported.
Return actual mounted tool metadata and mutating-tool visibility.
Return the authenticated token name and its effective scopes.
Return the Prometheus text exposition for authenticated scrapers.
Return success when the API process is running. No authentication.
Check that the metadata store is reachable and an embedding model is active. Returns 503 until both conditions hold. No authentication.

BlacklistKind.title: BlacklistKind
BlacklistKind.description: Whether a blacklist rule matches a URL exactly or a domain suffix.

ClusterProjectionPointResponse.title: ClusterProjectionPointResponse
ClusterProjectionPointResponse.description: A page point in a clustering run's display projection.

ClusterRef.title: ClusterRef
ClusterRef.description: Cluster membership shown on a result (populated from M4).

CompareAgreement.title: CompareAgreement
CompareAgreement.description: Agreement stats for one model pair.

ComparedPage.title: ComparedPage
ComparedPage.description: One ranked page in a compare arm.

FeatureStatus.title: FeatureStatus
FeatureStatus.description: Status of an asynchronous enrichment feature for a page.

ForgetRequest.title: ForgetRequest
ForgetRequest.description: POST /v1/forget body: exactly one of url/domain.

IngestBatchAcceptedResult.title: IngestBatchAcceptedResult
IngestBatchAcceptedResult.description: A newly queued item in an ingest batch.

IngestBatchBlacklistedResult.title: IngestBatchBlacklistedResult
IngestBatchBlacklistedResult.description: A blacklisted item in an ingest batch.

IngestBatchRejectedResult.title: IngestBatchRejectedResult
IngestBatchRejectedResult.description: An independently invalid item in an ingest batch.

IngestBatchRequest.title: IngestBatchRequest
IngestBatchRequest.description:
POST /v1/pages/batch envelope.

Items remain unvalidated here so one malformed page does not reject the
otherwise valid envelope. The route validates each item independently.

IngestBatchRevisitResult.title: IngestBatchRevisitResult
IngestBatchRevisitResult.description: A revisit item in an ingest batch.

JobKind.title: JobKind
JobKind.description: Kinds of durable jobs the queue executes.

JobRetryBatchRequest.title: JobRetryBatchRequest
JobRetryBatchRequest.description: POST /v1/jobs/retry body: explicit ids or a dead-job selector.

JobRetryBatchRetriedResult.title: JobRetryBatchRetriedResult
JobRetryBatchRetriedResult.description: A job that was reset to pending and re-enqueued.

JobRetryBatchSkippedResult.title: JobRetryBatchSkippedResult
JobRetryBatchSkippedResult.description: A job skipped because it is not dead.

JobStatus.title: JobStatus
JobStatus.description: Ledger status of a durable job.

Mediation.title: Mediation
Mediation.description: What mediates 'similar': vectors, cluster co-membership, or entities.

ModelReport.title: ModelReport
ModelReport.description: Mean metrics over one model's scored runs.

PageChunkResponse.title: PageChunkResponse
PageChunkResponse.description: One stored chunk with its position in the page body.

PageStatus.title: PageStatus
PageStatus.description: Lifecycle of a page through the indexing pipeline.

PageStatusBatchFoundResult.title: PageStatusBatchFoundResult
PageStatusBatchFoundResult.description: Status for a page known to the server.

PageStatusBatchMissingResult.title: PageStatusBatchMissingResult
PageStatusBatchMissingResult.description: Status result for an unknown or expired page id.

RollupStrategy.title: RollupStrategy
RollupStrategy.description:
How chunk scores pool into a page score.

No magic-number bonuses in v1: rerankers are calibrated, and arbitrary
boosts would destroy their ranking. A bonus variant may be added later
only if eval proves lift.

SearchBatchErrorResult.title: SearchBatchErrorResult
SearchBatchErrorResult.description: One batch query that failed with a per-item (non-fatal) error.

SearchBatchOkResult.title: SearchBatchOkResult
SearchBatchOkResult.description: One successful batch query with its full search response.

SearchBatchRequest.title: SearchBatchRequest
SearchBatchRequest.description: POST /v1/search/batch body: shared params + up to 20 query strings.

Suggestion.title: Suggestion
Suggestion.description: A related page appended to the response.

UpdateWatchRequest.title: UpdateWatchRequest
UpdateWatchRequest.description:
PATCH /v1/watches/{watch_id} body; omitted fields stay unchanged.

``url`` is immutable: it is the watch's dedup identity — delete and
recreate to point a watch elsewhere.

WatchKind.title: WatchKind
WatchKind.description: Kinds of pull sources a watch can poll.

WatchResponse.title: WatchResponse
WatchResponse.description: One watch with its schedule and last-poll health.

WatchStatus.title: WatchStatus
WatchStatus.description: Outcome of a watch's most recent poll.

WhoAmIResponse.title: WhoAmIResponse
WhoAmIResponse.description: Authenticated token identity and effective scopes.
